questions.question: Who is the narrator of the story?
questions.answer: The narrator is Anita, a fourteen-year-old girl.

questions.question: Who is Anand and how old is he?
questions.answer: Anand is Anita’s younger brother, and he is thirteen years old.

questions.question: What does Anand consider himself to be?
questions.answer: Anand considers himself to be a scientist and an inventor.

questions.question: Why did Anand decide to invent a burglar alarm?
questions.answer: He decided to invent it because there had been a series of thefts in their colony.

questions.question: What happened to Papa when he entered the house?
questions.answer: He got soaked with water from a water bag that was part of Anand's burglar alarm.

questions.question: How was the burglar alarm supposed to work?
questions.answer: It was designed to ring a bell and drop a water bag when the door was opened gently.

questions.question: What movie inspired Anand’s next big project?
questions.answer: The movie 'Back to the Future' inspired him.

questions.question: What was Anand's second major invention?
questions.answer: His second major invention was a time machine.

questions.question: Where did Anand build his time machine?
questions.answer: He built it in his own bedroom.

questions.question: What did Anita warn Anand about while helping him?
questions.answer: She warned him not to involve her if their inventions got them into trouble.

questions.question: Why did Papa hide his toolbox?
questions.answer: He hid it to prevent Anand from taking more tools for his messy experiments.

questions.question: Where did the parents go for one night?
questions.answer: They went to a wedding in Kharagpur.

questions.question: What promise did Anand make to his parents before they left?
questions.answer: He promised not to touch or use the time machine while they were away.

questions.question: Who entered the house while the parents were away?
questions.answer: A man named Boppa, who was a ruffian/sweeper in the neighborhood.

questions.question: How did Boppa threaten the children?
questions.answer: He threatened them with a stick and demanded to know where their father’s room was.

questions.question: Where did Anand tell Boppa to go?
questions.answer: He tricked Boppa into going into his own room (the one with the time machine).

questions.question: What strange noises came from Anand’s room after Boppa entered?
questions.answer: There were clanging, humming, and thumping sounds.

questions.question: Who came to help the children?
questions.answer: Neighbors like Mr. and Mrs. Sharma and Dr. Mohan came to help.

questions.question: What did the neighbors find when they opened Anand's room?
questions.answer: They found the time machine humming, but Boppa was gone.

questions.question: What object belonging to Boppa was left behind?
questions.answer: His green scarf was left near the time machine.

questions.question: What did Anand believe happened to Boppa?
questions.answer: Anand believed that Boppa had been transported through time by the machine.

questions.question: What did Papa do with the time machine eventually?
questions.answer: He dismantled it because he thought it was unsafe.

questions.question: What was Anand’s next project at the end of the story?
questions.answer: He started building a telephone to talk to aliens.

questions.question: What does 'tinkering' mean in the story?
questions.answer: It means playing with or trying to fix gadgets in a creative way.

questions.question: Is Anand discouraged by his failed inventions?
questions.answer: No, he remains confident and moves quickly on to his next idea.
